Empires & Puzzles Suomuhauki Review: The 5-Star Legendary Nature Green Hero from Kalevala Family

Forget simple snipers; Suomuhauki is a fish-man on a mission. With a Pike Mega Fiend that deals massive damage and cripples attack stats, this Kalevala legend is a nightmare for Fire heroes. We dive deep into his mechanics, stats, and best strategies.
Suomuhauki Review: The Hulking Pike That Bites Back
Suomuhauki: The Hulking Pike That Swallowed The Meta

Suomuhauki arrives from the Legends of Kalevala event (July 2025) looking like something that crawled out of a fisherman's worst nightmare—or perhaps his best catch. He is a Fast mana speed Nature (Green) sniper who brings a unique and devastating mechanic to the table: the

. While many snipers just hit and run, Suomuhauki hits and leaves behind a parting gift that keeps on taking. He is specifically designed to hunt Fire heroes, turning their elemental advantage into a liability. If you've ever wanted to slap an enemy with a giant fish, this is your chance.

The artwork is... certainly memorable. We have a very buff, very humanoid body topped with an incredibly realistic, slightly terrifying pike head. He wields a trident that looks like it's seen better days (probably spent at the bottom of a lake). The color palette is swampy greens and purples, fitting his aquatic nature. It's a bold design choice that leans heavily into the "monster" aspect of the Kalevala mythos. It's not "cool" in the traditional sense, but it is undeniably imposing. You wouldn't want to meet him in a dark pond.

Suomuhauki boasts stats that are very much in line with the modern power curve of mid-to-late 2025. With a base Power of 1224 and an Attack stat of 1306, he hits hard. While the very latest heroes from December 2025 (like the Magic Tower or Astral heroes) might edge him out slightly in raw Power (hovering around 1260), Suomuhauki's stats are still top-tier. His defense is respectable, but his massive health pool (2261) ensures he can take a hit, which is crucial for surviving long enough to deploy his aquatic parasites.
This skill is deceptive. On paper, "400% damage to target" at Fast speed is good, but standard. The real magic—and the terror—is the
every turn. With Suomuhauki's high base attack, this is not a tickle; it's a chunk of health disappearing every round.
Against Fire characters, the Fiend deals "strong damage" and imposes a harsher attack debuff (-55% vs -45%). This makes him a direct counter to the red heroes that usually threaten green teams.

. This is a massive amount of healing required to remove it. For a hero with 3000 HP, healers need to pump out 2100 HP worth of healing just to get rid of the fish.

Fast. He needs 8 tiles to charge, or 7 with a standard mana troop (level 29 magic/styx or lower with costume/bard bonuses), and 6 tiles is achievable with the right combination of 20% mana node + bard/costume bonuses + troops, making him a "6-tile" ghost in Rush situations or specific raid setups.
1306 Attack x 400% = ~5224 raw damage value (before defense mitigation). This is a solid snipe.

If a target has 2500 Max HP, the Fiend absorbs 1750 Healing. That effectively "silences" a healer's output for that hero for multiple turns.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Kalevala family bonus and passives add significant survivability to this offensive powerhouse.
The damage reduction from the stronger element (Fire, in this case) is excellent. It allows Suomuhauki to survive a hit from a Red sniper that would normally vaporize a Green hero.

is a clutch mechanic. Recovering 15% health and receiving 15% mana when dropping to low health can turn a losing battle into a win. It often gives him just enough juice to fire off one last Special Skill before dying.
is situational but useful in the current meta where Fiends are common. Being immune to new Fiends for 6 turns means he won't be bogged down by enemy summoners early in the match.
Released in July 2025, Suomuhauki represented a significant step up for the Kalevala family, bringing "Mega Fiend" mechanics to a seasonal event.
Despite being ~6 months old, Suomuhauki has aged like fine wine. His stats are still within 5% of the absolute newest heroes. His mechanic (Mega Fiend) is arguably
valuable now as health pools have increased, making percentage-based interactions powerful. The heavy attack debuff (-45%) ensures he remains relevant as a defensive tool as well.

He is a staple in offensive War teams (especially against Red tanks or flanks) and holds his own in defenses due to the sheer pressure the Mega Fiend exerts. He is not just a collector's item; he is a weapon.
Suomuhauki's primary role is damage. His initial hit is decent, but the Fiend's damage scales off his
stat. Therefore, there is only one correct path.
Maximizes the initial snipe damage. More importantly, it maximizes the turn-by-turn damage of the Pike Mega Fiend, which is his unique selling point.
Slightly squishier, but the Kalevala family bonus mitigates this against Fire enemies.
without hesitation. Every point of Attack adds to the nightmare he summons.
The Pike Mega Fiend is one of the most oppressive ailments in the game. It deals massive damage and requires immense healing to remove.
He flips the script on the elemental triangle, punishing Red heroes with extra damage and debuffs.
The -45% (-55% vs Fire) Attack debuff neuters the target, making them hit like a wet noodle while the fish chews on them.
The Power of Sisu passive often saves him from death.
He is a sniper. If the board goes bad, he can only affect one enemy at a time.
Heroes that prevent minion/fiend summoning (like Lunar New Year heroes or other specific blockers) completely shut down half his kit.
While the Fiend absorbs healing, a team with
heavy healers might be able to clear it, though it will cost them their specials to do so.
Excellent for taking out high-threat targets or tanks. Even if you don't kill them instantly, the Fiend often finishes the job.
A top-tier sniper for picking apart defenses.
High attack stat and high tile damage make him solid, though the Fiend animation eats time (irrelevant for tile damage but good to note).
The Fiend's debuff is a "status effect," so it counts as a negative for enemies, but doesn't directly interact with Buff Booster rules for
team. However, his sheer speed and damage are always good.
Almur (Elemental Def Down) -> Suomuhauki -> Another Green Sniper/Hitter.
Almur softens them up, Suomuhauki hits for massive damage and plants the Fiend. If the target survives, the Fiend kills them next turn.
Overwhelming the enemy with Fiends makes it impossible for them to heal back up.
Heroes who block Fiends (e.g., Garnet, Arco) are his worst nightmare.

Fiends from enemies (rare, but be careful with mechanics that might "reset" enemy states if any exist).

If you pulled him, congratulations. Level him immediately. He will be your primary boss killer. The Fiend damage is percentage-based (270% of his attack), which is often more effective than your other heroes' special skills early on.

He is worth the Limit Break (LB1) and potentially the Alpha Aether (LB2). The "Fiend Resist" alpha power is nice, but the stat boost is the real draw. In the current meta of massive overheads and boosted health, a mechanic that
70% of max health is one of the few ways to reliably grind down an overhealed tank.
The community reaction to Suomuhauki was a mix of amusement at his design and awe at his power.
"The King of Pikes needs to see how his baby works." — A player embracing the fish theme.
"This video is just reading the card... It would be nice to see some action instead." — Early frustration with lack of gameplay footage.
"Is this the most hyped hero in 2025? The whole concept of a fish head man is epic to begin with." — Users loving the bizarre art direction.
Many noted that the Mega Fiend is capable of overwriting Mega Minions, a crucial tactical advantage in late 2025 wars.

Suomuhauki is a fantastic addition to the Nature roster. He isn't just a damage dealer; he's a problem creator. He forces the enemy to deal with the Pike Fiend or die trying. With modern stats, a game-changing debuff, and a design that you definitely won't forget, he is a keeper. Don't let this one swim away.
A top-tier sniper who remains highly relevant.
